common.name: Name
common.label: Label
common.extractedVarsSaved: Extracted Variables → saved to project variables

projects.title: Project management
projects.intro: <0>Superadmins</0> create and delete projects. <1>Project admins</1> edit project details, environments, and members for that project. <2>Members</2> can view only.
projects.sidebarHint: Your sidebar shows <0>Superadmin</0> (platform) and/or <1>Project admin</1> (at least one project with the admin role). Project-level roles are managed in the members table below.
projects.emptySuperadmin: No projects yet. Use “New project” to create one.
projects.emptyMember: No projects yet. Ask a superadmin to add you or create a project.
projects.deleteConfirm: Delete project "{{name}}"? This cannot be undone.
projects.envHintManage: Set a base URL per environment (e.g. https://api.example.com).
projects.envHintView: Only superadmins and project admins can edit environment URLs.
projects.addMemberHint: Users must be registered. Separate multiple emails with <0>;</0>.
projects.selectProjectDetail: Select a project to view details

admin.title: Platform admin
admin.intro: Global users, superadmin flags, and account activation. For projects, environments, and project-level members, use <0>Project management</0>. Project admins manage those within each project; superadmins can create or delete projects.

llm.title: LLM Management
llm.subtitle: Configure the LLM model used by AI orchestration, and review token consumption records and trend charts.
llm.selectModelError: Please select or enter a model name
llm.envHint: API Base URL and API Key are configured via backend environment variables <0>OPENAI_API_BASE</0> and <0>OPENAI_API_KEY</0>. Supports OpenAI, OpenRouter, and other compatible APIs.
llm.noUsageData: No usage data yet. Trend charts will appear after using AI orchestration.
llm.noRecords: No usage records yet. Records will appear after using AI orchestration.

apis.varsDialogSubtitle: Reference in URL / Headers / Body with {{syntax}} · Current env: {{env}}
apis.noFolders: No folders yet. Create one to start.
apis.selectEndpoint: Select an endpoint or create a new one
apis.deleteFolderConfirm: Delete this folder and all its endpoints?
apis.extractIntro: Automatically extract values from the response after sending a request and store them as project variables. Reference them in URL / Headers / Body with {{syntax}}.
apis.assertionsIntro: Add assertions to verify the response. Pass/fail results are shown after sending the request.
apis.curlPlaceholder:
Paste your cURL command here…

curl -X POST https://api.example.com/users \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name":"John"}'
apis.openapiPlaceholder: Paste OpenAPI JSON or YAML spec here…

orch.noBaseUrlWarn: No Base URL configured; relative path requests will fail
orch.relativePrefix: Relative paths will be prefixed with: {{url}}
orch.configureBaseUrl: Please configure a Base URL for this environment in Project settings
orch.noEndpointsHint: No API endpoints found. Go to API Management to add some.
orch.aiDialogBody: Describe your test scenario in natural language. The AI will automatically select appropriate endpoints from your project's API library and generate a complete test flow (including parameter overrides, variable extraction, and assertions). You can fine-tune the result on the canvas after generation.
orch.aiPromptPlaceholder: e.g. Test user registration → login → get profile flow
orch.aiWorking: AI is analyzing endpoints and orchestrating the test flow…
orch.clickNodeHint: Click a node to enter a custom step name
orch.stepNamePlaceholder: Give this step a descriptive name…
orch.overrideIntro: Leave empty to use the original values from the API library. URL / Headers / Query / Body all support {{varSyntax}}: Project Variables from the current run environment, auto-generated {{runUuidSym}} / {{runMsSym}} per run (useful for unique emails), and variable names defined in upstream Extract rules.
orch.overrideBodyType: Body Type defaults to JSON. If you only override the body text without changing the type, it will be sent as JSON. If you don't override the body at all, the original body and type from the library will be used. Select "Use original" to keep everything as-is.
orch.useOriginalWarn: "Use original" selected: the body type from the API library will be used. If it's set to None and you've entered a body above, please switch to JSON.
orch.extractIntro: After execution, extract values from the response using JSONPath and store them in the variable context for downstream steps to reference via {{sym}}.
orch.assertIntro: Add assertions to this step's response. If any assertion fails, the step is marked as failed. Expected values support {{sym}} to reference upstream extracted variables.
orch.handleDragTarget: Drag here from another step's bottom to connect
orch.handleDragSource: Drag from here to another step's top to connect
orch.canvasHelpConnect: Connect: Drag from a node's bottom handle to another node's top handle.
orch.canvasHelpEdit: Edit node: Click a node to open Overrides / Extracts on the right; click Save in the toolbar when done.
orch.canvasHelpRemoveEdge: Remove connection: Select an edge and press Delete / Backspace, or double-click the edge to remove it.

orchAiExamples: Test full flow: user registration → login → get profile, using a unique email for signup
Verify the CRUD flow: create project, update project name, then delete project
Test that accessing a protected endpoint without login returns 401
